author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
title: The Fifth Passage.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “This,” says he, “is another text of theirs: ‘Who will boast that he has a pure heart?’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And then he answered this with several passages, wishing to show that there can be in man a pure heart.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But he omits to inform us how the passage which he reported as quoted against himself must be taken, so as to prevent Holy Scripture seeming to be opposed to itself in this text, and in the passages by which he makes his answer.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: We for our part indeed tell him, in answer, that the clause, “Who will boast that he has a pure heart?” is a suitable sequel to the preceding sentence, “whenever a righteous king sits upon the throne.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For how great soever ever a man’s righteousness may be, he ought to reflect and think, lest there should be found something blameworthy, which has escaped indeed his own notice, when that righteous King shall sit upon His throne, whose cognizance no sins can possibly escape, not even those of which it is said, “Who understandeth his transgressions?”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “When, therefore, the righteous King shall sit upon His throne, . . . who will boast that he has a pure heart? or who will boldly say that he is pure from sin?”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Except perhaps those who wish to boast of their own righteousness, and not glory in the mercy of the Judge Himself.
